examples.text: لَنْ أَنَامَ حَتَّى يَكُفَّ ٱلطِّفْلُ عَنِ ٱلْبُكَاءِ.
examples.english: I will not sleep until the child stops crying.

senses.gloss: to curb, to contain, to constrict
to hinder, to keep somebody off from something

examples.text: وَهُوَ ٱلَّذِى كَفَّ أَيْدِيَهُمْ عَنكُمْ وَأَيْدِيَكُمْ عَنْهُم بِبَطْنِ مَكَّةَ مِنۢ بَعْدِ أَنْ أَظْفَرَكُمْ عَلَيْهِمْ ۚ وَكَانَ ٱللَّهُ بِمَا تَعْمَلُونَ بَصِيرًا
examples.english: And it is He who withheld their hands from you and your hands from them within [the area of] Makkah after He caused you to overcome them. And ever is Allāh, of what you do, Seeing.

senses.gloss: palm of the hand, the inner surface of the hand from wrist to finger tips

examples.text: وَأُحِيطَ بِثَمَرِهِ فَأَصْبَحَ يُقَلِّبُ كَفَّيْهِ عَلَىٰ مَا أَنفَقَ فِيهَا وَهِيَ خَاوِيَةٌ عَلَىٰ عُرُوشِهَا وَيَقُولُ يَا لَيْتَنِي لَمْ أُشْرِكْ بِرَبِّي أَحَدًا

senses.gloss: verbal noun of كَفَّ (kaffa) (form I) – abstaining, abstention, cessation, desistance, discontinuation, refraining, stoppage, suspension

etymology.text: From Proto-Semitic *kapp-. Cognate with Hebrew כף (kaf).
